mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals emerging labor market slack that reduces consumer spending and corporate profit margins, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55% in backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and real sales of goods indicates synchronized aggregate demand growth that boosts corporate earnings, producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns in > 50% of triggered months, or rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.02 during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with rising policy rates creates stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and raises borrowing costs, driving negative weekly returns.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or signal accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a downturn in construction and related consumer spending, reducing forward earnings expectations and driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or threshold signal shows no predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production rises while payroll growth stagnates indicates capital deepening and productivity gains that boost profit margins, yielding positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence signal predicts negative returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term loses significance (p > 0.10) in multivariate models.
